nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Hey Jade, you're still alive."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Four hundred and twenty-and-a-half. Could be twenty-one, depending on how you count the small one in the shallow grave by the well back in the village."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "What?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Four hundred and twenty-two?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] How did you know? That's literally my favorite number."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Well, you're crazy. I'm out of here."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text:
John doesn't look up, his thumb running over a notch on a piece of driftwood.
"Forgive me, friend. Usually, people around here are either screaming or bleeding. I just like to keep a tally of the travelers I've had to bury since the Verge broke.

Four hundred and twenty... well, at least the last time I checked. Keeps the mind sharp."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Hey Stranger, maybe you can give me some information instead of a grave."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] Wait a second... Wait a fucking second... are you implying I'm going to be number four-twenty-two?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] You've been here a long time. Since the Verge War, I take it?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 7] This was the Mayor's estate. Why the hell are you still squatting in the ruins of authority?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 5] Sorry to disappoint, old man, but I'm not planning on dying today."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Weak] Look, maybe you could help me fight? I can pay you."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Information? Sure. I've got plenty of that. Most of it's useless though"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He chuckles, a tired sound. "To be straight with you, I'm a farmer’s son. Only reason I'm not next is because I'm already dead. Don't feel bad only because you're a dead man walking."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Verge War... That's what they call it now?" He shakes his head. "Suppose it sounds better than 'the great chewing.' I was a soldier, yeah. Marched out there thinking the gods had a grand plan for us. Turns out they were on holiday."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He looks at the mansion and shrugs. "The Mayor? He had 'important business' elsewhere the second things got ugly. I stayed because I like the quiet. And the wine cellar was still full."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Fair enough. Perhaps you're different, perhaps you aren't"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text:
He looks at your coin pouch, then at you, unimpressed.
"Coin's not worth much when the merchants are buried over there."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text:
He looks you up and down with a mild grimace.
"You've got that restless look about you. The sort who goes actively looking for trouble instead of just waiting for it to find you like a sensible person."

He lets out a long sigh.
"Name's John. Since I'll be the one finding a quiet patch of dirt for you later, you might as well tell me who you are."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "The name is Jade."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I'm Jade. I came here to kill those monsters."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Intelligence 5] I'm Jade. And I'm not planning on staying in that tally of yours."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 5] I'm Jade. I'm the one who's going to make sure you stop counting at four-twenty."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text:
He lets out a soft breath that seems like a mild laugh.
"Jade... it's a hard stone. Let's hope you don't break easily.

Well, Jade, ask your questions. I've got nothing better to do these days."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "What exactly happened here?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "If it's so bad, why didn't you leave with the others? Why stay here?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I could use some help. Join me."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I'll leave you to your counting."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "The short version? The ground opened up. The long version involves a lot of screaming, a lot of fire, and the Verge shattering into this pathetic pile of rocks."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "The village over there was a nice quiet little place, however turns out the monsters were obsessed with the crystal veins running under the island. We didn't have a chance really'"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: For the first time, he has a sad look in his eyes."Never figured out why. Suppose even monsters like shiny things. I'm not sure. They don't talk much."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " I'm a farmer by trade and a soldier by bad luck. Neither of those professions taught me how to swim, and I don't trust boats."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Besides, my brother would not have liked to see me run. We aren't much of the quitting type."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He pats the patch of grass next to him."Someone had to stay behind to tend the graves. It ain't much of a retirement plan, but the neighbors are quiet."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He shakes his head."Flattering, but no. I'm not as strong as I was in my youth... and I wasn't even strong then"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He nods. "Try not to die, Jade. I'm running out of room."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Dumb] But you are so old, are you sure the monsters won't be afraid of your face?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Charisma 5] I don't need a god. I just need some help. What's the worst that could happen, we finally die for real?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Weak] Look at me! I'm as good as number four-twenty-one without your help."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "[Strength 5] I'll do most of the killing, don't worry"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Never mind then. I'll leave you to your counting."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He stares at you for a long, painful moment."You aren't particularly smart, right?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He laughs."I suppose that's true."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He grimaces."You're not wrong. But I don't know if I want to be number four-twenty-two."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He raises an eyebrow."You might actually have a shot, I can see that."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He lets out a long, ragged sigh and finally tosses his piece of driftwood into the grass."Fine. If the gods actually gave a damn about my retirement, they wouldn't have sent someone this stubborn to ruin it. Let's go kill those bastards before I come to my senses."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He leans his weight against his weapon and lets out a long sigh."I was just thinking that fighting again isn't quite as fun as it used to be. Then I remembered it was never actually fun to begin with. What do you need, Jade?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I need you to do something."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I need to ask you something."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Nothing new, stay on guard."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Let's keep moving."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He stretches his neck, "I knew there was a catch to this partnership."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He shrugs his heavy shoulders."Go ahead. I'm practically an encyclopedia of useless history."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He rests his hand on his hilt."Always am. Hard to sleep anyway with all the screaming. I'll watch your back."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Wait here. Don't move until I get back."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "I think we should part ways. I'm going on alone."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Stay close to me, don't wander off."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Never mind. Forget I asked."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Fine by me. Gives me a chance to relax. Try not to die while I'm not looking, alright?"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Alright, Jade. I'll head back to that cool house I have."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Sure, I don't like to walk much anyways."

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "What exactly happened here?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "If it's so bad, why didn't you leave with the others?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Any advice on fighting these monsters?"

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: "Never mind, I don't have any questions right now."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "The short version? The ground opened up. The long version involves a lot of screaming, a lot of fire, and the Verge shattering into this pathetic pile of rocks."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "The village over there was a nice quiet little place, however turns out the monsters were obsessed with the crystal veins running under the island. We didn't have a chance really"

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: For the first time, he has a sad look in his eyes."Never figured out why. Suppose even monsters like shiny things. I'm not sure. They don't talk much."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: " I'm a farmer by trade and a soldier by bad luck. Neither of those professions taught me how to swim, and I don't trust boats."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: "Besides, my brother would not have liked to see me run. We aren't much of the quitting type."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He pats the patch of grass next to him."Someone had to stay behind to tend the graves. It ain't much of a retirement plan, but the neighbors are quiet."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He rubs his chin."Hit them until they stop moving. I think that's kinda the universal way of killing bastards. But you know the saying, a big bastard dies like any other bastard."

nodes.speaker: npc
nodes.text: He gestures forward with his free hand."Lead the way, boss. I'll be right behind you, complaining quietly."
